hypothesis: If aromatic valency is computed correctly (accounting for 1.5 bond order) and the GEOM-Drugs dataset is kekulized to remove aromatic ambiguity during training, then retrained 3D molecule generators will produce structures with higher valency validity and lower GFN2-xTB relaxation and geometry deviations than when trained on the original dataset because corrected bonding constraints align model learning with chemically accurate structure rules.
